name: Idomeneus
description: Idomeneus, a Cretan king and grandson of Minos, was a prominent figure in the Trojan War. He led the Cretan forces with bravery and skill, contributing significantly to the Achaean cause. Known for his wisdom and tactical acumen, he was a respected leader amongst the Greek warriors. Idomeneus was one of the suitors of Helen and thus bound by oath to defend Menelaus's claim. During the war, he distinguished himself in numerous battles, slaying many Trojan warriors. However, his journey home was fraught with peril. Caught in a fierce storm, Idomeneus vowed to Poseidon that if he returned safely to Crete, he would sacrifice the first living thing he encountered. Upon landing, he was tragically greeted by his own son. Idomeneus, bound by his oath, reluctantly sacrificed his son, a deed that brought plague and devastation to Crete. Some accounts say he was then banished, while others suggest he ruled for a time before departing for Italy. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of rash vows and the tragic consequences of divine intervention. Idomeneus represents the complex interplay of heroism, piety, and the burdens of leadership in Greek mythology. His legacy is one of both valor and tragedy, reminding us of the human cost of war and the potentially devastating power of the gods.
subtitle: Cretan King, Trojan War Hero
achievements: Leading the Cretan contingent in the Trojan War
Bravery and skill in battle against the Trojans
Participating in the sack of Troy
Surviving the perilous journey home from Troy

keyMyths.title: Idomeneus in the Trojan War
keyMyths.description: Idomeneus led the Cretan forces with valor, fighting alongside the Achaeans against Troy. He was a skilled warrior and strategist, earning respect from his peers. His contributions were vital to the Greek war effort.

keyMyths.title: Idomeneus's Vow and Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: During a storm at sea, Idomeneus vowed to sacrifice the first living thing he saw upon returning home. Tragically, this was his son, leading to dire consequences for Crete. This event highlights the dangers of rash promises to the gods.

keyMyths.title: Idomeneus's Exile or Departure
keyMyths.description: Following the sacrifice of his son, Idomeneus faced plague and unrest in Crete. Some accounts say he was banished, while others state he left voluntarily for Italy. His fate remains a subject of varied interpretations.
keyMyths.source: Servius' commentary on Virgil's Aeneid, Strabo's Geography

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Primary source detailing Idomeneus's role in the Trojan War.
sources.text: Primary source detailing Idomeneus's role in the Trojan War.

sources.author: Virgil
